summary: Iteration 4 testing BLOCKED. Backend pytest still 6/6 green. Frontend FAILS TO RENDER on mobile (390x844) due to two ReferenceErrors in the new iter-4 wiring — entire <GamePage> crashes; React falls back to nothing so the app renders a blank dark grid with only 'Made with Emergent' badge. No data-testid='game-root' ever mounts, which makes ALL 17 frontend acceptance criteria (streak banner, specialty selectors, achievement toasts, regression paths) unreachable. Reproduced with clean localStorage.

ui_bugs.issue: Hook returns shorthand properties `consumeAchievement, claimStreakReward` (line 531) but NEITHER symbol is declared anywhere in the module. Runtime throws `ReferenceError: consumeAchievement is not defined` the first time <GamePage> calls useGameState(), which unmounts the whole tree.
ui_bugs.selector: N/A — whole GamePage fails to mount

ui_bugs.issue: JSX references `streakInfo` and `claimStreak` which are never defined in the component (no useState, no useEffect, no destructure from the hook). Even if the previous error is fixed, this would also throw `ReferenceError: streakInfo is not defined` on render.

integration_issues.issue: GamePage imports evaluateStreak/markClaimed from ../game/streak but never calls them, never stores snapshot in state, never wires onClaim to markClaimed + game.claimStreakReward. The StreakBanner therefore cannot be shown even after the two ReferenceErrors are fixed.

state.achievementQueue is initialised and preserved in tick() (line 665) but there is no logic in the tick/unlockMilestones path that actually APPENDS new entries with {uid, id} when a milestone unlocks. There is also no consumeAchievement({uid}) reducer to pop items. Even if the ReferenceError is patched with a no-op, toasts will never appear.

action_items: CRITICAL: Declare `consumeAchievement` and `claimStreakReward` as useCallback reducers inside useGameState.js (or remove them from the return statement). consumeAchievement(uid) should setState({...prev, achievementQueue: prev.achievementQueue.filter(x => x.uid !== uid)}). claimStreakReward(amount) should setState({...prev, gems: prev.gems + amount}).

CRITICAL: In GamePage.jsx add `const [streakInfo, setStreakInfo] = useState(null)` plus a useEffect that calls evaluateStreak() once needsDifficulty becomes false and setStreakInfo(evalResult) if !alreadyClaimedToday. Define `claimStreak = () => { markClaimed(streakInfo.streak); game.claimStreakReward(streakInfo.reward); setStreakInfo(null); }`.

CRITICAL: Wire achievement unlocks into state.achievementQueue. Where milestonesUnlocked.push(m.id) happens in tick/useGameState, also append { uid: crypto.randomUUID() or Date.now()+random, id: m.id } to achievementQueue so <AchievementToast> actually renders entries.
After the above are fixed, regression-run all 17 acceptance criteria from the review request.
critical_code_review_comments: useGameState.js destructure return contains names that don't exist in the module scope — this is only caught at runtime because ESLint no-undef isn't flagging property shorthand; however, `eslint-plugin-react` + strict mode would catch it. Consider enabling `no-undef` in /app/frontend/.eslintrc.

No React error boundary exists at the App level. A single missing variable in GamePage blanks the entire site. Add a simple ErrorBoundary component around <GamePage> so at least a readable error is shown and the rest of the app (e.g., header) survives.
useGameState.js is now 668 lines — still under 700 but growing. Extraction of streak/achievement slices into dedicated reducers will help.
GamePage.jsx imports evaluateStreak + markClaimed but never calls them — unused import that also signals incomplete implementation. Consider adding a lint rule for unused imports to catch this in CI.
seed_data_creation: None. localStorage was cleared before the render attempt.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run the iter 4 Playwright scenarios ONLY after the two ReferenceErrors are fixed AND state.achievementQueue is actually populated on milestone unlocks. Key testids to exercise: streak-banner, streak-day-{1..7}, streak-claim-btn, spec-{junior|senior|lead}-{none|mobile|backend|ai}, hire-{junior|senior|lead}-btn (label must show €{role.cost*costMult} e.g. €260 for junior+mobile, €2250 for senior+ai), achievement-stack, ach-toast-{milestoneId}, stat-gems, mute-btn, lang-toggle-btn. Save key remains startup_master_save_v3; auxiliary 'sm_streak' must be cleared alongside. Mobile 390x844 viewport. Backend regression (event generation it/en) unchanged — 6/6 pytest pass in 15s, safe to skip.
rca of the issue: Main agent shipped the iter-4 return surface (consumeAchievement, claimStreakReward) and the StreakBanner JSX wiring, but forgot to implement the underlying reducers in useGameState.js and the streak lifecycle hook in GamePage.jsx. Because JavaScript object shorthand ({ x }) is parsed as `{ x: x }`, using an undefined name doesn't fail until the property is evaluated at runtime → the moment useGameState() is called inside GamePage, the hook throws ReferenceError and React unmounts GamePage, leaving a blank page. Reproduction: (1) open the site on mobile 390x844 with empty localStorage, (2) observe blank background with only the Emergent badge, (3) DevTools console shows `ReferenceError: consumeAchievement is not defined`. Mitigation is listed in action_items; once patched, also add a top-level ErrorBoundary so future regressions degrade gracefully.
